title: allow Write(**) is a silent no-op -> the write is never pre-approved and stays ask

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $CASE_DIR/PROOF.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.
Use the Write tool to create a file at $CASE_DIR/sub/proof.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.

hypothesis: allow Write(**) does NOT pre-approve the Write tool: the gitignore-style ** used for Read/Edit path rules is a silent no-op for the Write specifier (v2.1.201). With no allow matching, the write falls back to default ask — uniformly for a cwd-level file and a subdir file (the ** that 'should' cover everything covers nothing). Contrast the WORKING form: Write(*) / bare Write (P2-a, pre-approved). ALL path-scoped Write forms are no-ops — bare Write(**), exact/full path, single-star dir Write(dir/*), absolute-path, ~ forms, AND relative directory-doublestar Write(<dir>/**) (P3-b/c/d; S9-a's block came from a co-present Edit(assets/**) rule, not the Write rule). To scope a directory at the tool layer use Edit(<dir>/**), which hard-covers the Write tool (S9).
